text:
Once upon a time, in the peaceful forest of Greenwood, there lived a curious squirrel named Oliver.

Oliver loved exploring. One sunny afternoon, he decided to climb up the oldest oak tree in the forest. The journey was long and winding.

Suddenly, Oliver spotted something unusual: a tiny mouse wearing a tiny hat.

"Woah! You have wonderful taste!" murmured Oliver.

The mouse giggled.

"I wear it for ghosts. They hate hats."

"Oh. Well, I hate mice."

"You hate mice? But we're friends!"

"No. You listen. I read about ghost moles. They fear hats."

"Oh."

They floated down safely.

One day, a big owl swooped down.

"Oh no! You!"

"It's me. Oliver?"

Just like clockwork. The owl swooped down.

"Poor owl, you frighten easily."

"But you like clocks. Do you own one?"

"Just a big old globe."

Oliver giggled.

"You should have a hat!"
